sections.text:
**PREVIRTV**

**Read and ask questions.**

**Healthy or not?**
Do you have a healthy body?
Exercise and good food make your body healthy.

Exercise gives you a strong body.
You can walk to school or ride a bike.
You can go to the park and play.

**Eunie**
Fruit and vegetables are good for your health.
It's good to eat meat and fish too.
You should also drink milk every day.

----

sections.title: 第 17 页
sections.text:
Explore
Healthy or not?

Tingting: Look at this poster, Xiaowen, It's about healthy habits.
Xiaowen: Cool, Let's see. How much water do you drink every day, Tingting?
Tingting: I drink very little water. I need to drink more.
Xiaowen: Me too.
Tingting: Xiaowen, we eat too much junk food! We must eat less.
Xiaowen: That's hard! But we can try.
Tingting: I think I get enough exercise. I do a lot of sport.
Xiaowen: I need to do more exercise.

Healthy habits


Xiaowen: Drink a lot of water. Do exercise. Get enough sleep.
Eat healthy food. Don't eat junk food.

**Communicate**

**Listen and circle, Then say.**

**Health tips for children**
by Doctor Li

1. Do half an hour/an hour of exercises every day.

2. Don't play on the computer/play with friends all day.

3. Eat fruit and vegetables/meat and noodles every day.

4. Don't eat unhealthy food too often, or you may get ill.

5. Drink six/eight glasses of water every day.

6. Milk/Juice is good for you too.

7. Get 6-8/8-10 hours of sleep a night.

----

sections.title: 第 28 页
sections.text:
**PREVIEW**

Read and complete.

**Two cities in different countries**

**Countries around the world**

**Venice**
Venice is a city in Italy. It is by the sea. There are many bridges in Venice, but no roads. People get around on foot or by boat on the water.

**Suzhou**
Suzhou is a city in China. Like Venice, it has many bridges. There are beautiful gardens too. Suzhou has both old and new buildings.

**Venice**  **Suzhou**

What's fun about it?

sections.title: 第 29 页
sections.text:
Explore

Countries around the world

China is in the east of Asia. Beijing is the capital of China. You can find museums, palaces, and parks there. Most tourists like visiting these places. They like visiting the Great Wall too. People in Beijing love eating Beijing roast duck.

sections.title: 第 30 页
sections.text:
The UK is in the northwest of Europe. London is the capital of the UK. In London, you can find many museums and old buildings. Tourists like walking across Tower Bridge and visiting the British Museum. Fish and chips is popular in London.

France is in the west of Europe. Paris is the capital of France. In Paris, you can find many museums. Tourists like visiting the Louvre Museum and the Eiffel Tower. Bread and wine are popular in Paris.

What can you learn about these countries?

sections.title: 第 35 页
sections.text:
**Life in different countries**

To Adam
Subject: Life in Chengdu

Hi!
My name is Li Wei. I'm 12 years old. I'm from China.
I live in Chengdu. It's in the southwest of China. There are lots of things to do here. We like going to the teahouses. We sometimes go to see the pandas at the panda base. We love the cute pandas.
I'm in Grade Five. There's a Sichuan Opera club in our school. I learn Sichuan Opera in the club after school. My sister and I love hot pot.
Please write and tell me about yourself.
Yours,
Li Wei

sections.title: 第 36 页
sections.text:
To Li Wei
Subject: Life in Vancouver

Hello!

My name is Adam and I'm from Canada. I'm 11 years old. I live in Vancouver. It's in the west of Canada. There are mountains near here. We often go skiing there in winter.

I'm in Grade Six. I like hockey. I often watch it on TV. Sometimes my parents and I go to a game together. My favourite food is pancakes.

Yours,
Adam

sections.title: 第 41 页
sections.text:
Explore

A holiday plan
Shasha: It'll be the school holiday soon! Where shall we go?
Dad: Let's go to Yan'an.
Shasha: How will we get there?
Mum: We'll take the high-speed train.
Jinjin: I love trains! What will we do there?
Dad: We can explore Yan'an Revolutionary Memorial Hall. We'll visit the pagoda on Pagoda Hill. We'll take a bus to visit the Hukou Waterfall. It'll take us about three hours.
Shasha: Where will we stay in Yan'an?
Mum: We'll stay in a cave house!
Jinjin: Wow! Will there be good food?
Dad: Yan'an is famous for deep-fried cakes.
Shasha: It sounds great. I can't wait to go!

About Yan'an

Yan'an is in Shaanxi Province. It is a wonderful city.

Things to do:

- See the views on Pagoda Hill
- Learn about China's history at Yan'an Revolutionary Memorial Hall
- See the Hukou Waterfall on the Yellow River
- Enjoy deep-fried cakes
- Stay in a cave house

sections.title: 第 47 页
sections.text:
Holidays up high

Mountains are tall and beautiful. Many people like to visit them on their holidays. Here are three amazing mountains.

Mount Taishan

Mount Taishan is a wonderful mountain in China. Visitors can see caves and waterfalls. The tallest peak is Jade Emperor Peak. Visitors climb it to watch the sunrise.

sections.title: 第 48 页
sections.text:
Mont Blanc is in France and Italy. In summer, visitors hike in the mountains; in winter, visitors go skiing on the snow.

Mount Qomolangma is in China and Nepal. It is the world's tallest mountain and is very hard to climb.

Would you like to spend your holidays in these mountains?

sections.title: 第 52 页
sections.text:
**PREVIEW**
Read and write.

My toy bear is small and brown. It does not eat or drink, It does not grow.

Living and non-living things

This baby bear lives in the forest. It is big and brown. It likes eating food.

**Toy bear**  **Baby bear**

Is it big or small?
What colour is it?
Does it eat or drink?
Does it grow?

**Answer the question before you read.**
Can you name some living and non-living things?

sections.title: 第 53 页
sections.text:
Explore 
Living and

We see many things in our world every day. All these things are living or non-living. How are they different?

Living things
A living thing can grow and change. A plant is a living thing. An animal is a living thing. You are a living thing! Living things need air, food, and water. People and animals can move. They can get food and water from different places. Plants cannot move by themselves. They make their food from sunlight, water, and air. What living things do you see every day?

sections.title: 第 54 页
sections.text:
**Non-living Things**

A non-living thing does not need air, food, or water. A toy is a non-living thing. A non-living thing does not grow, but it can change. Look at the shoes. How are they different?

A ball can move. Is it a living thing? No, it isn't. It cannot move by itself. It does not grow. Look around you. What non-living things do you see?

All living and non-living things in our world are important. We should take care of them.

How are living and non-living things different?

sections.title: 第 59 页
sections.text:
**Extend**

**Living to non-living**

Some living things can become non-living things. Is the boy's straw hat from a living thing?

Wheat grows in fields.

Farmers cut the wheat.

It becomes straw. The sun dries the straw.

People can make a hat with the straw.

Straw begins as a living thing: wheat. People make it into a hat. The wheat becomes a non-living thing.

sections.title: 第 60 页
sections.text:
Grandma Lihos has a bamboo steamer. She makes boozl in it. How can we make a steamer?

Bamboo can grow very tall.

People cut down the bamboo.

People cut the bamboo into pieces.

People turn the bamboo pieces into a steamer.

Bamboo begins as a living thing. People make a steamer with the bamboo pieces. The bamboo becomes a non-living thing.

sections.title: 第 61 页
sections.text:
**Part 1**
- People make a hat with the straw.
- Wheat grows in fields.
- Farmers cut the wheat.
- The sun dries the straw.

**Part 2**
- People cut the bamboo into pieces.
- People cut down the bamboo.
- People turn the bamboo pieces into a steamer.
- Bamboo grows tall.

----

**Think**
What other non-living things come from living things?
How are they the same? How are they different?

**Think more about the Big Question**
What are living things?

**Living things**
- can grow and change.
- need air, food, and water.

1. What is the main idea?

Circle these words in "Natural resources" and guess.

hurt      factories  sick


Answer the question before you read.
What do you know about natural resources?

sections.title: 第 65 页
sections.text:
The Earth gives us important things. We call these things natural resources. Air, water, wood, and land are all natural resources.

**Why we need natural resources**

Living things need clean air and clean water to live. Plants need water and land to grow, and we need plants for food. We also make a lot of things with wood, like paper, chairs, and houses.

sections.title: 第 66 页
sections.text:
**How We Hurt Our Natural Resources**

We should not hurt our natural resources; we need them to live. Smoke from cars and factories makes the air dirty. Dirty air can make people sick. Rubbish makes the land and water dirty. We cannot drink dirty water, and we cannot use dirty land.

**How We Should Take Care of Our Natural Resources**

We should all take care of the Earth's natural resources. Here are some ideas:
- Use things longer.
- Reduce the things we use.
- Recycle our rubbish.

**What Can We Do Today?**

Why should we take care of our natural resources?

sections.title: 第 67 页
sections.text:

1. The girl has a cold. She is hurt.
2. People make goods in these places: factories.
3. Strong light can hurt our eyes.

We should not hurt our natural resources.

Details            Details               Details

How can we take care of our natural resources?

We should

Let's all make the Earth a better place.

sections.title: 第 77 页
sections.text:
Explore Important festivals 新春大吉

To Katie
Subject: Spring Festival

Dear Katie,
I'm very excited! It'll be the Spring Festival next month. We'll clean our house. This is hard work, but it'll bring good luck. I'll help my grandmother make jiaozi; it's my favorite food. Then I'll watch a lion dance with my family. And we'll visit my aunt and uncle. We'll have a big meal together. What festivals do you celebrate?

Yours,
Li Na

This is HARD WORK, but it'll bring GOOD LUCK.

sections.title: 第 78 页
sections.text:
MERRY CHRISTMAS

To: LiNa
Subject: Christmas

Dear Li Na,
The Spring Festival sounds fun! In the UK, our main festival is Christmas. About one month before Christmas, we put up a Christmas tree at home. On Christmas Eve, I always put a stocking by my bed. The next morning, there are gifts in it! My grandparents come on Christmas Day. First, we open our gifts. Then, we eat a big meal.

Yours,
Katie

How do the children and their families celebrate festivals?

sections.title: 第 83 页
sections.text:
New Year's Day around the world

It will be New Year's Day in two days. How will you celebrate it? Let's find out how people around the world celebrate the New Year. It is always fun and exciting!

**China**
In China, we celebrate New Year's Day with our family and friends. We relax and have fun. We hope to have a good year.

**USA**
In the USA, people watch fireworks on New Year's Eve. On New Year's Day, many people watch American football matches.

sections.title: 第 84 页
sections.text:
Scotland

In Scotland, people visit their family and friends. They bring a piece of coal for good luck.

Spain

In Spain, people wait for the clock to ring at midnight. They eat 12 grapes—one for each chime.

We make and enjoy traditional food. We visit relatives and have a big meal together.

sections.title: 第 88 页
sections.text:
**CURRENT_PAGE_RAW_OCR_TEXT:**

PREVIEW
Read and circle.
How music makes us feel

Children all over the world play musical instruments. They can have a lot of different feelings when they play. At first, a lot of them feel excited. But then they need to practice every day, and sometimes they think playing music is boring.

Summary:
a Children can have different feelings when they play musical instruments.
b Children all over the world like playing musical instruments.

Answer the question before you read.
When you want to feel happy, what kind of music do you like to listen to?

sections.title: 第 89 页
sections.text:
Explore
How music makes us feel

People around the world enjoy music. Some music is fast, and some is slow. Listen to this piano music. Is it fast or slow?

Zhang Liang listens to slow music at night. Why?

Li Lin listens to fast music in the morning. Why?

Music and feelings
Different music makes us have different feelings. Slow music may make us feel relaxed, and fast music may make us feel excited. The same music can make one person feel happy and another unhappy.

sections.title: 第 90 页
sections.text:
Listen to this flute music. How does it make you feel?

Music and imagination

Music can help us imagine things. With fast music, we may imagine an animal running. With slow music, we may imagine an animal sleeping. Listen. What do you imagine the animals are doing? Will you listen to music at home today? What are you going to listen to? Why?

What do you imagine the animals are doing?

How does different music make us feel?

sections.title: 第 96 页
sections.text:
Lisa closes her eyes and plays. But the fast notes are difficult to play. "I can't play like Eric," she says. Mr. White plays some music on his computer. The fast notes sound like birds or rain. "That's beautiful. I'll play like that one day!"

Mr. White smiles. "Lisa, that's you playing!" "Really?" asks Lisa. Mr. White laughs. "You work hard and play well, but don't forget to enjoy the music. You're a great musician."

sections.title: 第 97 页
sections.text:
**Part 1:**
- a) Lisa is very worried. She does not think she can play like Eric at the concert.
- b) Lisa is unhappy. She does not want to play Eric's part at the concert.

**Part 2:**
- a) Lisa is very happy. She knows she will play like Eric one day.
- b) Mr. White tells Lisa to enjoy the music when she is playing.

**Think:** How do you feel when you play or listen to music?

**Think more about the Big Question:**
- How does music make us feel?
- Music makes us have different feelings.
- Music helps us imagine things.

sections.title: 第 98 页
sections.text:
**Project: An Interview**

**Think**
You are going to interview an adult about his/her holiday plan.
What questions will you ask? List the questions.

----

**Interview**
2 Interview an adult.

**Write**
Take notes about the interview.

----

**My Interview**
Me: Where will you go for holiday, Grandma?
Grandma: Your grandpa and I will go to the country.
Me: Sounds good. What will you do there?
Grandma: We'll do exercise every morning. Your grandpa will go fishing and I'll take photos with my friends. Of course, we'll eat a lot of healthy food.
Me: Great! And how long will you stay there?
Grandma: About two weeks.

----

**Project 1: An Interview**

My grandparents are going to the country. My grandpa will go fishing and my grandma will take photos with her friends.

Hi. Can you tell me about your interview? I interviewed my grandma. She's going to the country with my grandpa. They'll do exercise every morning. My grandpa will go fishing and my grandma will take photos with her friends. They'll eat a lot of healthy food. They'll stay there for two weeks.

sections.title: 第 102 页
sections.text:
**Unit Checklist**

**Tick the boxes.**
A. I can do this well.
B. I can do this.
C. I need help.

**Unit 1**
- I can talk about people's weekend plans.
- I can write an email about my weekend plan.
- I understand that I can do different things at the weekend.

**Unit 2**
- I can talk about my living habits.
- I can write some health tips.
- I understand how to keep healthy.

**Unit 3**
- I can talk about different countries.
- I can write about a country I want to visit.
- I understand what is fun about different countries.

**Unit 4**
- I can talk about my holiday plan.
- I can write about my plans for a holiday.
- I understand what is fun about going on holiday.

**Unit 5**
- I can talk about the differences between living and non-living things.
- I can write about a living or non-living thing.
- I understand how living and non-living things are different.

**Unit 6**
- I can talk about the things I should and shouldn't do to take care of the Earth.
- I can write about how to take care of the Earth.
- I understand why and how we should take care of the Earth.

**Unit 7**
- I can talk about different festivals.
- I can write about a plan for a festival.
- I understand that people around the world celebrate festivals in different ways.

**Unit 8**
- I can talk about the music I like in different situations.
- I can write about the music I listen to on a trip.
- I understand that music makes people have different feelings.
